reasoning:
1. Between the baseline date (March 30) and the first resolution date (April 6), the ECB will not hold any monetary policy meetings. Both dates fall within the same reserve maintenance period (begun March 12), which historically means extremely low volatility. Over March 2025, the daily €STR hovered at exactly 2.417 almost every day, drifting up to 2.418 roughly 20% of the time. We apply a ~24% baseline probability for April 6 to account for this random upward flutter.
2. For April 29, the equation fundamentally changes. The ECB meets on April 17. The macroeconomic consensus points heavily to an active rate-cutting cycle tracking toward a terminal rate of 1.75%-2.0% by mid-2025, with ~60-70% market odds of a 25 bps cut at the April meeting. Should they cut, the €STR will decisively drop to ~2.167 (ensuring a NO). If they pause, it will remain at ~2.417. Our probability drops to ~13%.
3. By June 28 and September 26, additional meetings (June 5, July 24, Sept 11) will have occurred, bringing the probability of the ECB holding the entire time near zero. The rate will almost certainly be substantially lower. 
4. However, observing the structural prompt instructions to increase uncertainty for distant horizons, and accounting for tail-end macroeconomic shocks resulting in subsequent future hiking cycles (like trade shocks causing a resurgence in European inflation by 2026), our probability shifts slowly upward for September and then substantially for March 2026.
